subcodes.title: Words to Mrs Moore, spoken on 1913-05-18
subcodes.first_line: The will-power exists both in animal and man. The animals enjoy only a physical will-power which is limited in the sphere of its activity... We develop our will-power through 'divine unrest' which causes us to long for the better and nobler ideals of life.
subcodes.subjects: @power of the mind over nature, @human soul as mirror; divine light, attributes within, @competition vs. cooperation; the struggle for existence, @human reality created in the image of God, @material and spiritual existence; two books, @man's distinction from the animal, @mission of the Manifestation of God in the world, @free will and predestination; fate
